_note: TKT-0052 / ADR-0136 build intake — the DEV-only live-model overlay for ui-agent-admin's chat preview. OUTSIDE-IN derived from the three zones the change touches (app package · dev proxy · site) + the gates; INSIDE-OUT derived independently from TKT-0052's Acceptance criteria and ADR-0136's Decision clauses. Encodes the LLD's fork resolutions (own resolver, prompt-projection wire representation, single-shot, multi-turn history, fail()-path error UX); the paired LLD (lld/agent-admin-live-model-overlay.lld.md) states each rationale.

nodes.label: app/.../agent-admin/entries.ts — composeLiveSystemPrompt(sections, capabilities, toolsEnabled): pure projection of the composed prompt + enabled capability entries (label/description/content) into ONE live system-prompt string; empty capability set ⇒ byte-identical to composeSystemPrompt(sections)

app/.../agent-admin/agent-admin-schema.ts — the seam types: AdminTurnRequest { text, system, model, history } · AdminAgentTurn = (req) => Promise<string> · AdminTurn { role, content } (app-local, structurally matched at the site layer — a2ui's tools-internal Turn is never imported)

agent-admin.ts — `agentTurn` non-reflected prop (the injectable construction site, the schema/store prop precedent): default undefined ⇒ the stub path; the component itself stays fetch-free, env-free, key-free

agent-admin.ts — #handleSubmit live fork: when agentTurn is set, fresh-read the store → AgentConfigSnapshot → composeLiveSystemPrompt → AdminTurnRequest → await the runner → handle.setNote(reply)+finalize(); a thrown runner → handle.fail(message); agentTurn undefined ⇒ today's stub branch byte-unchanged
agent-admin.ts — #history: AdminTurn[] (private): user text + reply text appended per completed turn on BOTH paths; passed to the live runner; never rewritten on a config change
agent-admin.md descriptor — the `agentTurn` property row (the contract trip-wire + derive-first API table read this)

a2ui/tools/agent/providers-config.ts — providerForModel(config, model): derive the owning IMPLEMENTED provider for a model id from providers.json (one registry source; no browser-side provider literal), then belt-and-braces resolvePair

a2ui/tools/agent/dev-proxy-plugin.ts — POST {MOUNT}/chat branch on the SAME mount (inserted BEFORE the existing generic POST, which stays byte-unchanged): body {system, model, messages} → providerForModel + resolvePair + env-key + providerFor (all existing helpers) → provider.stream() buffered server-side → ONE JSON {text}; errors → status JSON {error}, never a key

site/lib/admin-live-runner.ts — createAdminAgentTurn(): AdminAgentTurn-shaped: POST /__a2ui/agent/chat with an AbortSignal.timeout, !res.ok → throw the proxy's {error}; reuses live-proxy-transport.ts's probeLive (static import inside the dev-only dynamic graph)

site/pages/agent-admin.ts — wireLiveOverlay(): import.meta.env.DEV guard BEFORE a dynamic import('../lib/admin-live-runner.ts'); probeLive → assign admin.agentTurn + a live/stub status caption under the demo frame (page chrome)

package gates — entries.test.ts: projection content + empty-capability byte-equivalence; agent-admin.test.ts: injected stub runner → note rendered / thrown runner → fail path system bubble / fresh-read (store edit between turns reaches the next request's system+model) / history accumulation + non-rewrite

site gates — site/pages/agent-admin.test.ts: the a2ui-chat.test.ts source-level tree-shake gate verbatim (no static import of admin-live-runner/live-proxy-transport at module scope; DEV guard indexOf-precedes the dynamic import) + the SUPPORTED_MODELS ⊆ providers.json implemented-models drift trip-wire

tools gates — providers-config.test.ts: providerForModel (hit/miss/unimplemented-provider); dev-proxy chat-route validation shape (stub provider through the extracted/branch logic; the impure fetch path stays manual live acceptance, the SPEC-R3 adapter precedent)

actions.label: keep the static build's default path unchanged — #handleSubmit defaults to runStubAgentTurn, no live-call code in the built bundle (ADR-0131 cl.4/7 narrowed, not lifted)
prove the tree-shake construction with a standing gate (the SPEC-N2 discipline)
dispatch a REAL model turn from the chat canvas under vite dev
hold the ADR-0073 trust boundary — key server-side only, {provider,model} pair allowlisted, no second proxy stood up
project composeSystemPrompt's CURRENT output into the live request, fresh-read at turn time
project the selected SUPPORTED_MODELS id into the live request and validate it against the registry allowlist
give the four capability-entry kinds real wire representation (ADR-0136 Fork 3 resolved: system-prompt projection; toolsEnabled gates the tools kind)
render the live reply into ui-conversation (single-shot setNote/finalize under the frozen AgentTurnHandle contract)
degrade a network/provider failure visibly — an explicit error state, never a crash or silent swallow
show a busy/loading state while a live turn is in flight
reflect a mid-conversation config change on the NEXT turn only — no retroactive rewrite of prior turns
carry multi-turn context — prior turns replay into the live request
keep SUPPORTED_MODELS and providers.json in lockstep (a drift here is a live-only 400)
hold the layering law — no @agent-ui/router / @agent-ui/a2a import; the packaged component carries no fetch/env/proxy-URL
document the widened component contract in the shipped descriptor
surface live-vs-stub status to the dev user (probe result made visible)
derive the {provider,model} pair server-side from the ONE registry source (no hardcoded browser-side provider literal)
type the injectable turn-runner seam once, app-locally, without importing a2ui's tools-internal types
gate the pure route/pair logic deterministically (the impure fetch stays manual live acceptance)
gate the component's live fork + the projection's equivalence property deterministically

edges.why: the agentTurn prop is typed by the seam types
the site runner implements AdminAgentTurn's request/return shape (structural match)
the live fork composes the request's system string via composeLiveSystemPrompt
the fork branches on the prop the construction site exposes
the fork reads + appends the history record it sends
the descriptor documents the shipped prop
the chat route derives the pair through providerForModel before resolvePair
the runner POSTs the route's wire shape {system, model, messages} → {text}
the page dynamically imports the runner module
the page assigns the prop the component exposes
the equivalence/projection gates run against composeLiveSystemPrompt
the injected-runner behavior gates run against the live fork
the source-level tree-shake gate reads the finished page source
providerForModel's unit gate runs against the shipped helper
the route-validation gate runs against the shipped chat branch
